title: Putin faces a series of setbacks, fueling discontent in Russia and raising doubts about his strategy
subhed: While the Russian president claims that victory in Ukraine is within reach, Kyiv is stepping up drone strikes deep inside Russian territory. These military setbacks compound the country's mounting economic difficulties.

title: France experiences its hottest spring ever recorded

France inaugurates memorial to victims of Rwandan genocide
subhed: A double stele by artist Grada Kilomba, overlooking the Seine in Paris, was to be unveiled on Tuesday by French President Emmanuel Macron and Rwandan President Paul Kagame.

title: New study busts myth of the idle job seeker
subhed: A majority of job seekers immediately prioritize finding a job after registering with the employment agency, according to a new study by the unemployment insurance body. However, those returning to work often have to do with short-term contracts.

title: Ukraine's Kostyuk sets up clash with Russia's Andreeva in French Open semi-final
subhed: Marta Kostyuk beat compatriot Elina Svitolina in their three-set quarter-final on Tuesday. Earlier in the day, Mirra Andreeva breezed into the next round with a comprehensive defeat of Romania's Sorana Cirstea.

title: The 20 cities where France's wealthiest live
subhed: The city of Neuilly-sur-Seine outside Paris has the highest proportion of very wealthy households in France, according to a study by the Inequality Observatory published on Tuesday. The ranking is dominated by the western suburbs of Paris and the areas surrounding Geneva.

title: Trump weakens Netanyahu by forcing him to delay attack on Beirut
subhed: The US president ordered Israel not to attack the Lebanese capital. As with Gaza or Iran, only Donald Trump can restrain Israel's military force. This has caused frustration within the country's governing coalition, further weakening Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhed: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.
